title: MGL c.239 s.16 - Eviction record sealing
content:
Section 16: Eviction; petition to seal record; petition to access sealed record; consumer reporting agency; housing or credit screening applications; notice of satisfaction
Section 16. (a) For the purposes of this section, the following words shall have the following meanings unless the context clearly requires otherwise: "Consumer report", a written, oral or other communication of any information by a consumer reporting agency bearing on a person's credit worthiness, credit standing or credit capacity that is used or expected to be used or collected, in whole or in part, for the purpose of serving as a factor in establishing the person's eligibility for rental housing or other purposes authorized under section 51 of chapter 93. "Consumer reporting agency", an individual, partnership, corporation, trust, estate, cooperative, association, government or governmental subdivision or agency or other entity that, for monetary fees, dues or on a cooperative nonprofit basis, regularly engages, in whole or in part, in the practice of assembling or evaluating consumer credit information or other information on consumers for the purpose of furnishing consumer reports to third parties. "Court", the trial court of the commonwealth established pursuant to section 1 of chapter 211B and any departments or offices established within the trial court. "Court record", any paper or electronic records or data in any communicable form compiled by, on file with or in the care custody or control of, the court that concern a person and relate to the nature or disposition of an eviction action or a lessor action. "Eviction action", a summary process action under this chapter to recover possession of residential premises. "Lessor action", any civil action brought against the owner, manager or lessor of residential premises by the tenant or occupant of such premises relating to or arising out of such property, rental, tenancy or occupancy for breach of warranty, breach of any material provision of the rental agreement or violation of any other law. "No-fault eviction", an eviction action in which the notice to quit, notice of termination or complaint does not include an allegation of nonpayment of rent or violation of any material term of the tenancy by the tenant or occupant.
(b) A person having a court record of a no-fault eviction may petition the court to seal the court record at any time after the conclusion of the action, including exhaustion of all rights of appeal.
(c) A person having a court record in an eviction action for nonpayment rent may petition the court to seal the court record; provided that the action concluded not less than 4 years before the request and no eviction action for nonpayment or lessor action has been brought against the petitioner within the commonwealth in the 4 years preceding the request; and that the petitioner certifies the nonpayment of rent was due to an economic hardship.
(d) A person having a court record of a fault eviction may petition the court to seal the court record; provided that the action concluded not less than 7 years before the request and no eviction action for fault or lessor action has been brought against the petitioner within the commonwealth in the 7 years preceding the request.
(e) A person having a court judgment against them pursuant to section 19 of chapter 139 may petition the court to seal the court record; provided that the action concluded not less than 7 years before the request.
(e1/2) A person having a court record of an eviction action that resulted in a dismissal or final judgment in favor of the defendant may petition the court to seal the court record at any time after the conclusion of the action.
(f) Court records sealed under this section may, at the discretion of the court, be made available for public safety, scholarly, educational, journalistic or governmental purposes only.
(i) A consumer reporting agency shall not disclose the existence of, or information regarding, an eviction record sealed under this section. Information contained in a sealed court record shall be removed from the consumer report within 30 days of the sealing.
(j) An application used to screen applicants for housing or credit that seeks information concerning prior eviction actions shall include a statement that an applicant with a sealed record may answer "no record" to an inquiry relative to that sealed court record.
(k) A party who obtains a judgment or enters into an agreement in an eviction action solely for nonpayment of rent shall, not more than 14 days after satisfaction, file a notice of satisfaction with the court.
